page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server powered by AWS Graviton processors. It features 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each of the 192 vCPUs maps directly to a physical core, eliminating resource sharing at the execution level.

The system is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached options. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps, facilitating high-throughput data transfers.

The bare-metal architecture and high core density of this instance class offer qualitative cost efficiency for workloads requiring direct hardware access and high resource density without virtualization overhead. The 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio makes this server type suitable for general-purpose workloads, large-scale web serving, containerized microservices, and distributed applications that benefit from high-bandwidth networking and dedicated ARM-based compute resources.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.metal-48xl is a bare-metal general-purpose server featuring 192 dedicated physical cores built on the arm64 AWS Graviton architecture. Operating at 2.7 GHz with a 1.0 thread-to-core ratio, it provides 192 vCPUs without hyperthreading overhead. The system includes 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing a balanced 4.0 GB per core ratio. It lacks local storage and integrated GPUs, requiring network-attached storage solutions. Network performance is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 200 Gbps. This bare-metal configuration is designed for high-density deployments, containerized microservices, and general-purpose workloads requiring direct hardware access and high network throughput. It offers resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for applications that benefit from dedicated physical ARM cores and high-speed networking.
